tree.setup: You're meeting a landlord to discuss renting an apartment. You need to ask about the terms and negotiate.
tree.setup_zh: 你和房东见面，谈租房子的事情。你需要问清楚条件和价格。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这套房子一个月房租四千五，押一付三。你看看有什么问题吗？
turns.text_pinyin: zhè tào fángzi yī gè yuè fángzū sì qiān wǔ, yā yī fù sān. nǐ kànkan yǒu shénme wèntí ma?
turns.text_en: The monthly rent for this apartment is 4,500, with one month's deposit and three months' rent upfront. Do you have any questions?

options.text_zh: 太好了，谢谢张叔。那我下周过来签合同。麻烦您把涨租和换热水器的条件都写在合同里，这样双方都放心。
options.text_pinyin: tài hǎo le, xièxie zhāng shū. nà wǒ xià zhōu guòlái qiān hétong. máfan nín bǎ zhǎng zū hé huàn rèshuǐqì de tiáojiàn dōu xiě zài hétong lǐ, zhèyàng shuāngfāng dōu fàngxīn.
options.text_en: Great, thanks Uncle Zhang. I'll come sign the lease next week. Please include both the rent increase and the water heater replacement in the contract, so both sides are covered.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to ask about the rental conditions, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 房租包括水电费吗？合同签多长时间？另外这里交通方便吗？
options.text_pinyin: fángzū bāokuò shuǐdiànfèi ma? hétóng qiān duō cháng shíjiān? lìngwài zhèlǐ jiāotōng fāngbiàn ma?
options.text_en: Does the rent include utilities? How long is the contract? Also, is the transportation convenient here?
options.feedback: Covers three key practical questions — 合同 length, costs, and 交通方便. This is exactly what a savvy renter asks.

options.text_zh: 太好了，非常感谢你的帮助。希望这次没问题了。再见！
options.text_pinyin: tài hǎo le, fēicháng gǎnxiè nǐ de bāngzhù. xīwàng zhè cì méi wèntí le. zàijiàn!
options.text_en: Great, thank you so much for your help. I hope this one's fine. Goodbye!
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to ask about the rental conditions, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 水电费不包括，需要自己交。合同最少签一年。这里离地铁站走路十分钟，交通很方便。
turns.text_pinyin: shuǐdiànfèi bù bāokuò, xūyào zìjǐ jiāo. hétóng zuìshǎo qiān yī nián. zhèlǐ lí dìtiě zhàn zǒulù shí fēnzhōng, jiāotōng hěn fāngbiàn.
turns.text_en: Utilities are not included, you need to pay them yourself. The contract is for at least one year. It's a ten-minute walk to the subway station — transportation is very convenient.

options.text_zh: 我的优点是做事认真负责，善于和同事合作。我的缺点是有时候太注重细节，但我一直在努力改进。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ de yōudiǎn shì zuòshì rènzhēn fùzé, shànyú hé tóngshì hézuò. wǒ de quēdiǎn shì yǒu shíhou tài zhùzhòng xìjié, dàn wǒ yīzhí zài nǔlì gǎijìn.
options.text_en: My strength is being conscientious and responsible, and good at collaborating with colleagues. My weakness is sometimes paying too much attention to details, but I've been working to improve.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to negotiate or confirm the deal, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 张叔，我在这儿住了两年了，一直按时交房租，也没给您添过什么麻烦。一下子涨五百确实有点多。能不能少涨一点，比如涨两百？我还是很愿意继续住在这里的。
options.text_pinyin: zhāng shū, wǒ zài zhèr zhù le liǎng nián le, yīzhí ànshí jiāo fángzū, yě méi gěi nín tiān guò shénme máfan. yīxiàzi zhǎng wǔ bǎi quèshí yǒudiǎn duō. néng bù néng shǎo zhǎng yīdiǎn, bǐrú zhǎng liǎng bǎi? wǒ háishi hěn yuànyì jìxù zhù zài zhèlǐ de.
options.text_en: Uncle Zhang, I've lived here for two years, always paid rent on time, and never caused any trouble. Going up 500 all at once is really a lot. Could you raise it less, like 200? I'm happy to continue living here.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to negotiate or confirm the deal, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 条件我都了解了。房租能不能便宜一点？如果可以的话，我想尽快签合同。
options.text_pinyin: tiáojiàn wǒ dōu liǎojiě le. fángzū néng bù néng piányi yīdiǎn? rúguǒ kěyǐ dehuà, wǒ xiǎng jǐnkuài qiān hétóng.
options.text_en: I understand all the conditions. Could the rent be a bit cheaper? If so, I'd like to sign the contract as soon as possible.
options.feedback: Smart negotiation — shows you understood the 条件, makes a reasonable ask, and signals commitment with 签合同.

tree.cultural_note: In China, 押一付三 (one month deposit, three months rent upfront) is the most common rental payment structure. Negotiating rent is expected — landlords often set the initial price slightly high anticipating a counteroffer.
